testws 1.0.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • How To Build A Workshop Package
    • msqrob2PTM: msqrob2 Mass Spectrometry Data Analysis Workflow for differential PTM analysis
    • An Example Workshop

msqrob2PTM: msqrob2 Mass Spectrometry Data Analysis Workflow for differential PTM analysis

Lieven Clement, Nina Demeulemeester, Luca De Corso and Lucas Beerland

20 May 2026

Source: vignettes/msqrob2_workshop.rmd
msqrob2_workshop.rmd

Outline

Background

The human phosphorylation datasets consist of 47 samples from condition A and 43 from condition B. Two aliquots were processed for each sample: one dedicated to total proteome analysis and the other one to the phosphoproteome analysis. The main sample preparation steps were identical for proteomics and phosphoproteomics apart from the additional phosphopeptide enrichment step. Generated raw data files were searched against a database containing all human entries extracted from UniProtKB-SwissProt (25/08/2021, 20,339 entries) using MaxQuant (v.1.6.17). The minimal peptide length required was seven amino acids and a maximum of one missed cleavage was allowed. The mass tolerance for the precursor ions was set to 20 ppm for the first search and 4.5 ppm for the main search. The mass tolerance for the fragment ions was set to 20 ppm. For proteomics data, methionine oxidation and acetylation of proteins’ N-termini were set as variable modifications and cysteine carbamidomethylation as a fixed modification. For phosphoproteomics data, serine, threonine, and tyrosine phosphorylations were added as variable modifications. For protein quantification, the “match between runs” option was enabled. The maximum FDR was set to 1% at peptide and protein levels with the use of a decoy strategy. Intensities were extracted from the Evidence.txt file to perform the following statistical analysis. The dataset can be found on PRIDE (PXD043476).

Load packages 

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
library("QFeatures")  
library("dplyr") 
library("tidyr")
library("ggplot2")
library("msqrob2")    
library("stringr")
library("ExploreModelMatrix")
library("kableExtra")
library("ComplexHeatmap")
library("scater")
library("patchwork")
library("seqinr")
library("data.table")
library("BiocFileCache")
library("purrr")
})

Import data

The data were searched with MaxQuant version version 1.5.5.6 and are deposited on the PRIDE repository PXD043476.

In this chapter we use the search files available on msqrob2data.

Phospho Data

The phospho enriched data are stored in “evidence_phospho_enriched.txt.zip”. The corresponding annotation file can be found in “expDesign.csv”.

bfc <- BiocFileCache()
psmFile <- bfcrpath(bfc,"https://github.com/statOmics/msqrob2data/raw/refs/heads/main/dda/phospho/evidence_phospho_enriched.txt.zip")
psm <- fread(psmFile, check.names = TRUE, integer64 = "double")
head(psm)
##           Sequence Length Modifications                Modified.sequence
##             <char>  <int>        <char>                           <char>
## 1:   AAAIQTMSLDAER     13    Unmodified                  _AAAIQTMSLDAER_
## 2: AAARLSLTDPLVAER     15 Phospho (STY) _AAARLS(Phospho (STY))LTDPLVAER_
## 3: AAARLSLTDPLVAER     15 Phospho (STY) _AAARLS(Phospho (STY))LTDPLVAER_
## 4:   AAATPESQEPQAK     13 Phospho (STY)   _AAAT(Phospho (STY))PESQEPQAK_
## 5:   AAATPESQEPQAK     13 Phospho (STY)   _AAAT(Phospho (STY))PESQEPQAK_
## 6:   AAATPESQEPQAK     13 Phospho (STY)   _AAAT(Phospho (STY))PESQEPQAK_
##    Oxidation..M..Probabilities   Phospho..STY..Probabilities
##                         <char>                        <char>
## 1:                                                          
## 2:                             AAARLS(0.997)LT(0.003)DPLVAER
## 3:                             AAARLS(0.806)LT(0.194)DPLVAER
## 4:                                          AAAT(1)PESQEPQAK
## 5:                                          AAAT(1)PESQEPQAK
## 6:                                          AAAT(1)PESQEPQAK
##    Oxidation..M..Score.Diffs  Phospho..STY..Score.Diffs Acetyl..Protein.N.term.
##                       <char>                     <char>                   <int>
## 1:                                                                            0
## 2:                             AAARLS(26)LT(-26)DPLVAER                       0
## 3:                           AAARLS(6.2)LT(-6.2)DPLVAER                       0
## 4:                               AAAT(35)PES(-35)QEPQAK                       0
## 5:                               AAAT(35)PES(-35)QEPQAK                       0
## 6:                               AAAT(35)PES(-35)QEPQAK                       0
##    Oxidation..M. Phospho..STY. Missed.cleavages              Proteins
##            <int>         <int>            <int>                <char>
## 1:             0             0                0 sp|O75326|SEM7A_HUMAN
## 2:             0             1                1 sp|P13224|GP1BB_HUMAN
## 3:             0             1                1 sp|P13224|GP1BB_HUMAN
## 4:             0             1                0   sp|P49006|MRP_HUMAN
## 5:             0             1                0   sp|P49006|MRP_HUMAN
## 6:             0             1                0   sp|P49006|MRP_HUMAN
##         Leading.proteins Leading.razor.protein        Type  Raw.file Experiment
##                   <char>                <char>      <char>    <char>     <char>
## 1: sp|O75326|SEM7A_HUMAN sp|O75326|SEM7A_HUMAN MULTI-MATCH QX21595GM      BIO87
## 2: sp|P13224|GP1BB_HUMAN sp|P13224|GP1BB_HUMAN  MULTI-MSMS QX21643GM      BIO44
## 3: sp|P13224|GP1BB_HUMAN sp|P13224|GP1BB_HUMAN  MULTI-MSMS QX21643GM      BIO44
## 4:   sp|P49006|MRP_HUMAN   sp|P49006|MRP_HUMAN  MULTI-MSMS QX21469GM      BIO35
## 5:   sp|P49006|MRP_HUMAN   sp|P49006|MRP_HUMAN  MULTI-MSMS QX21484GM      BIO12
## 6:   sp|P49006|MRP_HUMAN   sp|P49006|MRP_HUMAN  MULTI-MSMS QX21487GM      BIO72
##    MS.MS.m.z Charge      m.z     Mass Uncalibrated...Calibrated.m.z..ppm.
##        <num>  <int>    <num>    <num>                               <num>
## 1:        NA      2 688.8456 1375.677                              2.1988
## 2:  832.4357      2 831.9324 1661.850                              2.6258
## 3:  555.2924      3 554.9573 1661.850                              1.3928
## 4:  704.3115      2 704.3112 1406.608                              1.3905
## 5:  704.8138      2 704.3112 1406.608                              1.4335
## 6:  704.3116      2 704.3112 1406.608                              1.3459
##    Uncalibrated...Calibrated.m.z..Da. Mass.error..ppm. Mass.error..Da.
##                                 <num>            <num>           <num>
## 1:                         0.00151460        -0.011460     -7.8943e-06
## 2:                         0.00218450         0.569050      4.7341e-04
## 3:                         0.00077295        -0.255910     -1.4202e-04
## 4:                         0.00097932        -0.040652     -2.8632e-05
## 5:                         0.00100960        -0.076414     -5.3819e-05
## 6:                         0.00094794         0.148200      1.0438e-04
##    Uncalibrated.mass.error..ppm. Uncalibrated.mass.error..Da.
##                            <num>                        <num>
## 1:                        2.1873                   0.00150670
## 2:                        3.1948                   0.00265790
## 3:                        1.1369                   0.00063093
## 4:                        1.3498                   0.00095069
## 5:                        1.3571                   0.00095579
## 6:                        1.4941                   0.00105230
##    Max.intensity.m.z.0 Retention.time Retention.length
##                  <num>          <num>            <num>
## 1:            689.3471         55.888          0.23706
## 2:            831.9327         71.062          0.42487
## 3:            554.9572         71.049          0.57573
## 4:            704.3113         26.579          0.55736
## 5:            704.3111         26.470          0.65098
## 6:            704.8127         26.531          0.48654
##    Calibrated.retention.time Calibrated.retention.time.start
##                        <num>                           <num>
## 1:                    55.209                          55.102
## 2:                    70.267                          69.982
## 3:                    70.255                          70.013
## 4:                    25.847                          25.650
## 5:                    25.794                          25.531
## 6:                    25.758                          25.626
##    Calibrated.retention.time.finish Retention.time.calibration
##                               <num>                      <num>
## 1:                           55.339                   -0.67882
## 2:                           70.407                   -0.79458
## 3:                           70.589                   -0.79458
## 4:                           26.207                   -0.73157
## 5:                           26.182                   -0.67618
## 6:                           26.112                   -0.77320
##    Match.time.difference Match.m.z.difference Match.q.value Match.score
##                    <num>                <num>        <lgcl>       <num>
## 1:              -0.35667          -0.00052413            NA      153.12
## 2:                    NA                   NA            NA          NA
## 3:                    NA                   NA            NA          NA
## 4:                    NA                   NA            NA          NA
## 5:                    NA                   NA            NA          NA
## 6:                    NA                   NA            NA          NA
##    Number.of.data.points Number.of.scans Number.of.isotopic.peaks   PIF
##                    <int>           <int>                    <int> <int>
## 1:                    11               7                        2    NA
## 2:                    32              13                        3     0
## 3:                    42              18                        4     0
## 4:                    32              18                        2     0
## 5:                    35              22                        2     0
## 6:                    24              16                        2     0
##    Fraction.of.total.spectrum Base.peak.fraction        PEP MS.MS.count
##                         <int>              <int>      <num>       <int>
## 1:                         NA                 NA         NA           0
## 2:                          0                  0 3.8590e-03           1
## 3:                          0                  0 6.7431e-05           1
## 4:                          0                  0 3.8898e-02           1
## 5:                          0                  0 3.8898e-02           1
## 6:                          0                  0 3.8898e-02           1
##    MS.MS.scan.number  Score Delta.score Combinatorics Intensity Reverse
##                <int>  <num>       <num>         <int>     <num>  <char>
## 1:                NA     NA          NA             0    886950        
## 2:             37853 72.417      53.094             2   4825400        
## 3:             37856 81.723      74.842             2   4500600        
## 4:             10391 69.602      53.712             2   2999900        
## 5:             10590 69.602      48.731             2   4720200        
## 6:             10171 69.602      56.248             2   3592100        
##    Potential.contaminant    id Protein.group.IDs Peptide.ID Mod..peptide.ID
##                   <char> <int>            <char>      <int>           <int>
## 1:                          51               106          3               3
## 2:                          52               323          4               4
## 3:                          53               323          4               4
## 4:                          54               449          5               5
## 5:                          55               449          5               5
## 6:                          56               449          5               5
##    MS.MS.IDs Best.MS.MS Oxidation..M..site.IDs Phospho..STY..site.IDs
##       <char>      <int>                 <char>                 <char>
## 1:                   NA                                              
## 2:        38         38                                           242
## 3:        39         39                                           242
## 4:        40         40                                           657
## 5:        41         41                                           657
## 6:        42         42                                           657
##    Taxonomy.IDs
##          <lgcl>
## 1:           NA
## 2:           NA
## 3:           NA
## 4:           NA
## 5:           NA
## 6:           NA

Filtering

We will already filter the data to make it smaller by removing psms with missing values for the intensities and psms for which the probability on a phospo group is smaller or equal to 75%.

  1. The phospho probability can be found in the variable Phospho..STY..Probabilities. It is indicated at the position of the modification in the sequence between brackets. If there is no phosphorylation site detected the variable contains an empty string. We return the maximum probability among all potential sites.

  2. We keep the peptidoforms out if they have a probability on a phosph group is larger than 0.75 and if their intensity is not NA.

psm <- psm |>
  mutate(
    prob = map_dbl(
      str_extract_all(Phospho..STY..Probabilities, "\\(([^)]+)\\)"),
      ~ if (length(.x) == 0) {
          0
        } else {
          max(as.numeric(str_remove_all(.x, "[()]")))
        }
    ) 
  ) |> filter(prob > .75 & !is.na(Intensity))

Annotation file 

annotFile <- bfcrpath(bfc,"https://github.com/statOmics/msqrob2data/raw/refs/heads/main/dda/phospho/expDesign.csv")
annot <- fread(annotFile, check.names = TRUE, integer64 = "double")
head(annot)
##             file  assay source subset
##           <char> <char> <char> <char>
## 1: QX21619GM.raw  BIO40     A1      y
## 2: QX21842GM.raw  BIO49    A10      y
## 3: QX21652GM.raw  BIO50    A11      y
## 4: QX21616GM.raw  BIO51    A12      y
## 5: QX21711GM.raw  BIO52    A13      y
## 6: QX21764GM.raw  BIO53    A14      y

The variable source can be used to link the phospho enriched and the non-enriched runs.

We will alter the annotation:

  1. We first generate variable runCol, that gives an overview of the different runs in the experiment, i.e. the unique names in the column Run of the report file. This is a mandatory column in the annotation file that is required by the readQFeatures function that will be used to generate a QFeatures object with the quant data. The run in the evidence file is indicated with the variable Raw.file and here does not contain the file extension .raw, which we remove from the filenames stored in File of the meta data.

  2. We make a variable condition. There are two conditions: condition A and condition B. We can extract this by taking the first letter from the variable source.

annot <- annot |> 
  mutate(
    runCol = gsub(pattern = ".raw",replacement = "", x=file), #1.
    condition = substr(source,1,1)
  )

Convert to QFeatures

First, recall that the psm table is file in long format. Every quantitative column in the precursor table contains information for multiple runs. Therefore, the function split the table based on the run identifier, given by the runCol argument (for MaqQuant, that identifier is contained in Raw.file). So, the QFeatures object after import will contain as many sets as there are runs. Next, the function links the annotation table with the PSM data. To achieve this, the annotation table must contain a runCol column that provides the run identifier in which each sample has been acquired, and this information will be used to match the identifiers in the Raw.file column of the psm table.

Here, we will use the Intensity column as quantification input.

(qf <- readQFeatures(assayData = psm,  
                              colData = annot,
                              quantCols = "Intensity",
                              runCol = "Raw.file"))
##   |                                                                              |                                                                      |   0%  |                                                                              |=                                                                     |   1%  |                                                                              |==                                                                    |   2%  |                                                                              |==                                                                    |   3%  |                                                                              |===                                                                   |   4%  |                                                                              |====                                                                  |   6%  |                                                                              |=====                                                                 |   7%  |                                                                              |=====                                                                 |   8%  |                                                                              |======                                                                |   9%  |                                                                              |=======                                                               |  10%  |                                                                              |========                                                              |  11%  |                                                                              |=========                                                             |  12%  |                                                                              |=========                                                             |  13%  |                                                                              |==========                                                            |  14%  |                                                                              |===========                                                           |  16%  |                                                                              |============                                                          |  17%  |                                                                              |============                                                          |  18%  |                                                                              |=============                                                         |  19%  |                                                                              |==============                                                        |  20%  |                                                                              |===============                                                       |  21%  |                                                                              |================                                                      |  22%  |                                                                              |================                                                      |  23%  |                                                                              |=================                                                     |  24%  |                                                                              |==================                                                    |  26%  |                                                                              |===================                                                   |  27%  |                                                                              |===================                                                   |  28%  |                                                                              |====================                                                  |  29%  |                                                                              |=====================                                                 |  30%  |                                                                              |======================                                                |  31%  |                                                                              |=======================                                               |  32%  |                                                                              |=======================                                               |  33%  |                                                                              |========================                                              |  34%  |                                                                              |=========================                                             |  36%  |                                                                              |==========================                                            |  37%  |                                                                              |==========================                                            |  38%  |                                                                              |===========================                                           |  39%  |                                                                              |============================                                          |  40%  |                                                                              |=============================                                         |  41%  |                                                                              |==============================                                        |  42%  |                                                                              |==============================                                        |  43%  |                                                                              |===============================                                       |  44%  |                                                                              |================================                                      |  46%  |                                                                              |=================================                                     |  47%  |                                                                              |=================================                                     |  48%  |                                                                              |==================================                                    |  49%  |                                                                              |===================================                                   |  50%  |                                                                              |====================================                                  |  51%  |                                                                              |=====================================                                 |  52%  |                                                                              |=====================================                                 |  53%  |                                                                              |======================================                                |  54%  |                                                                              |=======================================                               |  56%  |                                                                              |========================================                              |  57%  |                                                                              |========================================                              |  58%  |                                                                              |=========================================                             |  59%  |                                                                              |==========================================                            |  60%  |                                                                              |===========================================                           |  61%  |                                                                              |============================================                          |  62%  |                                                                              |============================================                          |  63%  |                                                                              |=============================================                         |  64%  |                                                                              |==============================================                        |  66%  |                                                                              |===============================================                       |  67%  |                                                                              |===============================================                       |  68%  |                                                                              |================================================                      |  69%  |                                                                              |=================================================                     |  70%  |                                                                              |==================================================                    |  71%  |                                                                              |===================================================                   |  72%  |                                                                              |===================================================                   |  73%  |                                                                              |====================================================                  |  74%  |                                                                              |=====================================================                 |  76%  |                                                                              |======================================================                |  77%  |                                                                              |======================================================                |  78%  |                                                                              |=======================================================               |  79%  |                                                                              |========================================================              |  80%  |                                                                              |=========================================================             |  81%  |                                                                              |==========================================================            |  82%  |                                                                              |==========================================================            |  83%  |                                                                              |===========================================================           |  84%  |                                                                              |============================================================          |  86%  |                                                                              |=============================================================         |  87%  |                                                                              |=============================================================         |  88%  |                                                                              |==============================================================        |  89%  |                                                                              |===============================================================       |  90%  |                                                                              |================================================================      |  91%  |                                                                              |=================================================================     |  92%  |                                                                              |=================================================================     |  93%  |                                                                              |==================================================================    |  94%  |                                                                              |===================================================================   |  96%  |                                                                              |====================================================================  |  97%  |                                                                              |====================================================================  |  98%  |                                                                              |===================================================================== |  99%  |                                                                              |======================================================================| 100%
## An instance of class QFeatures (type: bulk) with 90 sets:
## 
##  [1] QX21460GM: SummarizedExperiment with 273 rows and 1 columns 
##  [2] QX21466GM: SummarizedExperiment with 379 rows and 1 columns 
##  [3] QX21469GM: SummarizedExperiment with 301 rows and 1 columns 
##  ...
##  [88] QX21842GM: SummarizedExperiment with 152 rows and 1 columns 
##  [89] QX21845GM: SummarizedExperiment with 142 rows and 1 columns 
##  [90] QX21851GM: SummarizedExperiment with 452 rows and 1 columns

Non-enriched data 

psmFileNE <- bfcrpath(bfc,"https://github.com/statOmics/msqrob2data/raw/refs/heads/main/dda/phospho/evidence_nonenriched.txt.zip")
psmNE <- fread(psmFileNE, check.names = TRUE, integer64 = "double")

Annotation File 

annotFileNE <- bfcrpath(bfc,"https://github.com/statOmics/msqrob2data/raw/refs/heads/main/dda/phospho/expDesignNonEnriched.csv")
annotNE <- fread(annotFileNE,check.names = TRUE, integer64 = "double")

  1. We first generate variable runCol, that gives an overview of the different runs in the experiment, i.e. the unique names in the column Run of the report file. This is a mandatory column in the annotation file that is required by the readQFeatures function that will be used to generate a QFeatures object with the quant data. The run in the evidence file is indicated with the variable Raw.file and here does not contain the file extension .raw, which we remove from the filenames stored in File of the meta data.

  2. We make a variable condition. There are two conditions: condition A and condition B. We can extract this by taking the first letter from the variable Condition.

annotNE <- annotNE |> 
  mutate(
    runCol = gsub(pattern = ".raw",replacement = "", x=file), #1.
    condition = substr(source,1,1)
  )

The non-enriched dataset contains more samples than the phospho-enriched dataset. We will remove the samples that are not matching to the these in the phospho data. The samples can be matched by the variable source.

annotNE <- annotNE |> 
  filter(source %in% annot$source)

We will also filter out the psm-level data from the evidence file that does not match to runs corresponding to the samples that are retained. We can use the columns Raw.file in the psm table and runCol in the annotation for that purpose.

psmNE <- psmNE |> 
  filter(Raw.file %in% annotNE$runCol)

Convert to QFeatures

A gain we read the psm table which is in long format and use the Intensity column as quantification input.

(qfNE <- readQFeatures(assayData = psmNE,  
                              colData = annotNE,
                              quantCols = "Intensity",
                              runCol = "Raw.file"))
##   |                                                                              |                                                                      |   0%  |                                                                              |=                                                                     |   1%  |                                                                              |==                                                                    |   2%  |                                                                              |==                                                                    |   3%  |                                                                              |===                                                                   |   4%  |                                                                              |====                                                                  |   6%  |                                                                              |=====                                                                 |   7%  |                                                                              |=====                                                                 |   8%  |                                                                              |======                                                                |   9%  |                                                                              |=======                                                               |  10%  |                                                                              |========                                                              |  11%  |                                                                              |=========                                                             |  12%  |                                                                              |=========                                                             |  13%  |                                                                              |==========                                                            |  14%  |                                                                              |===========                                                           |  16%  |                                                                              |============                                                          |  17%  |                                                                              |============                                                          |  18%  |                                                                              |=============                                                         |  19%  |                                                                              |==============                                                        |  20%  |                                                                              |===============                                                       |  21%  |                                                                              |================                                                      |  22%  |                                                                              |================                                                      |  23%  |                                                                              |=================                                                     |  24%  |                                                                              |==================                                                    |  26%  |                                                                              |===================                                                   |  27%  |                                                                              |===================                                                   |  28%  |                                                                              |====================                                                  |  29%  |                                                                              |=====================                                                                         |=======================                                               |  32%  |                                                                              |=======================                                               |  33%  |                                                                              |========================                                              |  34%  |                                                                              |=========================                                             |  36%  |                                                                              |==========================                                            |  37%  |                                                                              |==========================                                            |  38%  |                                                                              |===========================                                           |  39%  |                                                                              |============================                                          |  40%  |                                                                              |=============================                                         |  41%  |                                                                              |==============================                                        |  42%  |                                                                              |==============================                                        |  43%  |                                                                              |===============================                                       |  44%  |                                                                              |================================                                      |  46%  |                                                                              |=================================                                     |  47%  |                                                                              |=================================                                     |  48%  |                                                                              |==================================                                    |  49%  | |========================================================              |  80%  |                                                                              |=========================================================             |  81%  |                                                                              |==========================================================            |  82%  |                                                                              |==========================================================            |  83%  |                                                                              |===========================================================           |  84%  |                                                                              |============================================================          |  86%  |                                                                              |=============================================================         |  87%  |                                                                              |=============================================================         |  88%  |                                                                              |==============================================================        |  89%  |                                                                              |===============================================================       |  90%  |                                                                              |================================================================      |  91%  |                                                                              |=================================================================     |  92%  |                                                                              |=================================================================     |  93%  |                                                                              |==================================================================    |  94%  |                                                                              |===================================================================   |  96%  |                                                                              |====================================================================  |  97%  |                                                                              |====================================================================  |  98%  |                                                                              |===================================================================== |  99%  |                                                                              |======================================================================| 100%
## An instance of class QFeatures (type: bulk) with 90 sets:
## 
##  [1] QE37617GM: SummarizedExperiment with 9912 rows and 1 columns 
##  [2] QE37620GM: SummarizedExperiment with 9672 rows and 1 columns 
##  [3] QE37623GM: SummarizedExperiment with 9806 rows and 1 columns 
##  ...
##  [88] QE37985GM: SummarizedExperiment with 7769 rows and 1 columns 
##  [89] QE37988GM: SummarizedExperiment with 6430 rows and 1 columns 
##  [90] QE37991GM: SummarizedExperiment with 6386 rows and 1 columns

Data processing

Phospho-enriched data

  1. Encoding missing values as zeros.
qf <- zeroIsNA(qf, names(qf))
  1. Keeping only the most intense PSM per ion (see here for a step-by-step explanation of the code). Upon this filtering every feature is unique to a ion identifier (peptide sequence + charge), and we hence can join sets using that identifier.
for (i in names(qf)) {
  rowdata <- rowData(qf[[i]]) 
  rowdata$ionID <- paste0(rowdata$Modified.sequence, rowdata$Charge) 
  rowdata$value <- assay(qf[[i]])[, 1]
  rowdata <- data.frame(rowdata) |>
    group_by(ionID) |>
    mutate(psmRank = rank(-value))
  rowData(qf[[i]])$psmRank <- rowdata$psmRank
  rowData(qf[[i]])$ionID <- rowdata$ionID
}
qf <- filterFeatures(qf, ~ psmRank == 1, keep = TRUE)
qf <- joinAssays(qf, names(qf), "ions", "ionID")
  1. Filtering out decoy sequences, potential contaminants and sigma proteins that were just used for QC purposes.
qf <- filterFeatures(qf, ~Proteins != "" & ## Remove failed protein inference
                       !grepl(";", Proteins) & ## Remove protein groups
                       Reverse != "+" & ## Remove decoys
                       Potential.contaminant != "+" & ## Remove contaminants
                       !grepl("Sigma", Proteins) ## Remove Sigma proteins used for internal QC
                     )
  1. log2-transform the intensities
qf <- logTransform(qf, base = 2, i = "ions", name = "ionsLog")

Only keep ions that are picked up in at least 10 samples.

n <- ncol(qf[["ionsLog"]])
qf <- filterNA(qf, i = "ionsLog", pNA = (n - 10) / n)
qf <- sweep(
  qf, MARGIN = 2, STATS = nfLogMedianOfRatios(qf,"ionsLog"), 
  i = "ionsLog", name = "ionsNorm"
)

QC 

psmDens <- qf[, , "ionsNorm"] |>
  longForm(colvars = colnames(colData(qf))) |> 
  data.frame() |> 
  filter(!is.na(value)) |>
  ggplot() + 
  aes(x = value,
      colour = interaction(condition, subset),
      group = colname) +
  geom_density() +
  theme_minimal() +
   labs(subtitle = "Normalised log2 phospho-ion intensities")
psmDens

qf[, , "ionsNorm"] |>
  longForm(colvars = colnames(colData(qf))) |> 
  data.frame() |> 
  filter(!is.na(value)) |>
  ggplot() + 
  aes(x = source,
      y = value,
      colour = interaction(condition,subset),
      group = colname) +
  xlab("sample") +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme_minimal() +
   labs(subtitle = "Normalised log2 phospho-ion intensities")

qf[, , "ionsNorm"] |>
  longForm(colvars = colnames(colData(qf)), rowvars = "Charge") |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  filter(!is.na(value)) |>
  filter(Charge<=4) |> 
ggplot(aes(x = source)) +
  geom_bar(aes(fill = factor(Charge, levels = 4:1)),
           colour = "black") +
  labs(title = "ion types per sample",
       x = "Sample",
       fill = "Charge state") +
  theme_bw()

qf[,,"ionsNorm"] |> 
  longForm(colvars = colnames(colData(qf)), 
           rowvars= c("ionID", 
                      "Proteins")) |>
  data.frame() |>
  filter(!is.na(value)) |>
  group_by(condition, source) |>
  summarise(ions = length(unique(ionID)),
            `Proteins` = length(unique(Proteins))) |> 
  pivot_longer(-(1:2),
               names_to = "Feature",
               values_to = "IDs") |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = source, y = IDs, fill = condition)) +
  geom_col() +
  #scale_fill_observable() +
  facet_wrap(~Feature,
             scales = "free_y") +
  labs(title = "Precursor and protein group identificiations per PTM sample",
       x = "Sample",
       y = "Identifications") +
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90))

psmMDS <- getWithColData(qf, "ionsNorm") |> 
  as("SingleCellExperiment") |> 
  runMDS(exprs_values = 1) |> 
  plotMDS(colour_by = "condition", shape_by = "subset") + 
  labs(title = "Median normalisation")
psmMDS

Non-enriched data

  1. Encoding missing values as zeros.
qfNE <- zeroIsNA(qfNE, names(qfNE))
  1. Keeping only the most intense PSM per ion (see here for a step-by-step explanation of the code). Upon this filtering every feature is unique to a ion identifier (peptide sequence + charge), and we hence can join sets using that identifier.
for (i in names(qfNE)) {
  rowdata <- rowData(qfNE[[i]]) 
  rowdata$ionID <- paste0(rowdata$Modified.sequence, rowdata$Charge) 
  rowdata$value <- assay(qfNE[[i]])[, 1]
  rowdata <- data.frame(rowdata) |>
    group_by(ionID) |>
    mutate(psmRank = rank(-value))
  rowData(qfNE[[i]])$psmRank <- rowdata$psmRank
  rowData(qfNE[[i]])$ionID <- rowdata$ionID
}
qfNE <- filterFeatures(qfNE, ~ psmRank == 1, keep = TRUE)
qfNE <- joinAssays(qfNE, names(qfNE), "ions", "ionID")
  1. Filtering out decoy sequences, potential contaminants and sigma proteins that were just used for QC purposes.
qfNE <- filterFeatures(qfNE, ~Proteins != "" & ## Remove failed protein inference
                       !grepl(";", Proteins) & ## Remove protein groups
                       Reverse != "+" & ## Remove decoys
                       Potential.contaminant != "+" & ## Remove contaminants
                       !grepl("Sigma", Proteins) ## Remove Sigma proteins used for internal QC
)
  1. log2-transform the intensities
qfNE <- logTransform(qfNE, base = 2, i = "ions", name = "ionsLog")

Only keep ions that are picked up in at least 10 samples.

n <- ncol(qfNE[["ionsLog"]])
qfNE <- filterNA(qfNE, i = "ionsLog", pNA = (n - 10) / n)
qfNE <- sweep(
  qfNE, MARGIN = 2, STATS = nfLogMedianOfRatios(qfNE,"ionsLog"), 
  i = "ionsLog", name = "ionsNorm"
)
qfNE <- aggregateFeatures(qfNE,
 i = "ionsNorm",
 fcol = "Proteins",
 na.rm = TRUE,
 name = "proteins",
 fun = MsCoreUtils::robustSummary)
##   |                                                                              |                                                                      |   0%
##   |                                                                              |======================================================================| 100%

QC 

psmDens <- qfNE[, , "ionsNorm"] |>
  longForm(colvars = colnames(colData(qfNE))) |> 
  data.frame() |> 
  filter(!is.na(value)) |>
  ggplot() + 
  aes(x = value,
      colour = interaction(condition, subset),
      group = colname) +
  geom_density() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(subtitle = "Normalised log2 ion intensities")
psmDens

qfNE[, , "ionsNorm"] |>
  longForm(colvars = colnames(colData(qfNE))) |> 
  data.frame() |> 
  filter(!is.na(value)) |>
  ggplot() + 
  aes(x = source,
      y = value,
      colour = interaction(condition,subset),
      group = colname) +
  xlab("sample") +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(subtitle = "Normalised log2 ion intensities")

qfNE[, , "ionsNorm"] |>
  longForm(colvars = colnames(colData(qfNE)), rowvars = "Charge") |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  filter(!is.na(value)) |>
  filter(Charge<=4) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = source)) +
  geom_bar(aes(fill = factor(Charge, levels = 4:1)),
           colour = "black") +
  labs(title = "ion types per sample",
       x = "Sample",
       fill = "Charge state") +
  theme_bw()

qfNE[,,"ionsNorm"] |> 
  longForm(colvars = colnames(colData(qfNE)), 
           rowvars= c("ionID", 
                      "Proteins")) |>
  data.frame() |>
  filter(!is.na(value)) |>
  group_by(condition, source) |>
  summarise(ions = length(unique(ionID)),
            `Proteins` = length(unique(Proteins))) |> 
  pivot_longer(-(1:2),
               names_to = "Feature",
               values_to = "IDs") |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = source, y = IDs, fill = condition)) +
  geom_col() +
  #scale_fill_observable() +
  facet_wrap(~Feature,
             scales = "free_y") +
  labs(title = "Precursor and protein group identificiations per PTM sample",
       x = "Sample",
       y = "Identifications") +
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90))

psmDens <- qfNE[, , "proteins"] |>
  longForm(colvars = colnames(colData(qfNE))) |> 
  data.frame() |> 
  filter(!is.na(value)) |>
  ggplot() + 
  aes(x = value,
      colour = interaction(condition, subset),
      group = colname) +
  geom_density() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(subtitle = "Normalised log2 protein intensities")
psmDens

qfNE[, , "proteins"] |>
  longForm(colvars = colnames(colData(qfNE))) |> 
  data.frame() |> 
  filter(!is.na(value)) |>
  ggplot() + 
  aes(x = source,
      y = value,
      colour = interaction(condition,subset),
      group = colname) +
  xlab("sample") +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(subtitle = "Normalised log2 protein intensities")

psmMDS <- getWithColData(qfNE, "proteins") |> 
  as("SingleCellExperiment") |> 
  runMDS(exprs_values = 1) |> 
  plotMDS(colour_by = "condition", shape_by = "subset") + 
  labs(title = "Median normalisation")
psmMDS

Differential Peptidoform abundance analysis

Model estimation

  1. We first define the model. We only have two sources of variability in the experiment that we can model, i.e. the effect of the condition and the subset.

  2. We fit this model to each psm ion of the normalised and log2-transformed assay in QFeature object qf using the msqrob function.

model <- ~ condition * subset
qf <- msqrob(
  qf,
  i = "ionsNorm",
  formula = model,
  robust = TRUE)

We abled M-estimation (robust = TRUE) for improved robustness against outliers.

Inference

We can now convert the research questions 1. Is a precursor DA between the conditions in subset x 2. Is a precursor DA between the conditions in subset y 3. Is the effect of condition different between subset x and y 4. Prioritise precursors that are marginally DA between conditions.

In other words, we need to translate each question in a null and alternative hypothesis using a single model parameter or a linear combination of model parameters, which is also referred to with a contrast.

To aid defining contrasts, we will visualise the experimental design using the ExploreModelMatrix package.

vd <- ExploreModelMatrix::VisualizeDesign(
    sampleData =  colData(qf),
    designFormula = model,
    textSizeFitted = 4
)
vd$plotlist
## [[1]]

colData(qf)$subset |> table()
## 
##  x  y 
## 30 60

ADD contrasts

Below we define the contrast and construct the corresponding contrast matrix.

contrast <- c("conditionB= 0", 
              "conditionB + conditionB:subsety = 0", 
              "conditionB:subsety = 0", 
              "conditionB + 60/90*conditionB:subsety = 0")
(L <- makeContrast(
  contrast,
   parameterNames = colnames(vd$designmatrix)
))
##                    conditionB conditionB + conditionB:subsety
## (Intercept)                 0                               0
## conditionB                  1                               1
## subsety                     0                               0
## conditionB:subsety          0                               1
##                    conditionB:subsety conditionB + 60/90 * conditionB:subsety
## (Intercept)                         0                               0.0000000
## conditionB                          0                               1.0000000
## subsety                             0                               0.0000000
## conditionB:subsety                  1                               0.6666667

We assess the contrast for each psm ion.

qf <- hypothesisTest(qf, i = "ionsNorm", contrast = L, overwrite = TRUE)

We extract the results table from the proteins summarised experiment in the qf object.

inferences <- 
  msqrobCollect(qf[["ionsNorm"]], L)
alpha <- 0.05
volcanoplot <- plotVolcano(inferences) + 
  facet_wrap(~contrast) + 
  labs(title = "peptidoform ion level")
volcanoplot

Data Modeling at protein-level (Robust Regression)

Model estimation 

qfNE <- msqrob(
  qfNE,
  i = "proteins",
  formula = model,
  robust = TRUE)
qfNE <- hypothesisTest(qfNE, i = "proteins", contrast = L, overwrite = TRUE)

We extract the results table from the proteins summarised experiment in the qf object.

inferencesNE <- 
  msqrobCollect(qfNE[["proteins"]], L)
alpha <- 0.05
volcanoplot <- plotVolcano(inferencesNE) + 
  facet_wrap(~contrast) + 
  labs(title = "protein level")
volcanoplot

Peptidoform usage 

calculate_usage_paired <- function(qf, qfNE, i_precursor, i_protein = "proteins", fcol_precursor = "Proteins", fcol_protein = "Proteins", name = "ion_usage", match_cols_precursor_to_protein = colnames(qf[[i_precursor]]))
{
    nf <- assay(qf[[i_precursor]])
    nf[,] <- NA
    match_precursor_to_protein_FC <- match(
      rowData(qf[[i_precursor]])[[fcol_precursor]], 
      rowData(qfNE[[i_protein]])[[fcol_protein]])
    idsMatch <- which(!is.na(match_precursor_to_protein_FC))
    nf[idsMatch, ] <- assay(qfNE[[i_protein]])[match_precursor_to_protein_FC[idsMatch], match_cols_precursor_to_protein]
    
    qf <- sweep( #4. Subtract log2 norm factor elementwise (MARGIN = 1:2)
      qf,
      MARGIN = 1:2,
      STATS = nf,
      i = i_precursor,
      name = name
      )
    match_name <- paste0("match_",i_precursor,"_to_",i_protein)
    rowData(qf[[name]])[[match_name]] <- match_precursor_to_protein_FC 
    qf <- filterFeatures(qf, i = name, filter = formula(paste0("~ !is.na(",match_name,")")))
}
matchedSamps <- match(colData(qf)$source,colData(qfNE)$source)
qf <- calculate_usage_paired(qf, qfNE, i_precursor = "ionsNorm", i_protein = "proteins", fcol_precursor = "Proteins", fcol_protein = "Proteins", name = "ion_usage", match_cols_precursor_to_protein = matchedSamps)
qf[, , "ion_usage"] |> #1.
  longForm(colvars = colnames(colData(qf))) |>  #2. 
  data.frame() |> 
  filter(!is.na(value)) |>
  ggplot() + #3.
  aes(x = value,
      colour = interaction(condition,subset),
      group = colname) +
  geom_density() +
  labs(subtitle = "usage") +
  theme_minimal()

Model Estimation 

qf <- msqrob(
  qf,
  i = "ion_usage",
  formula = model,
  robust = TRUE,
  overwrite = TRUE)

We assess the contrast for each phospho-precursor usage.

qf <- hypothesisTest(qf, i = "ion_usage", contrast = L, overwrite = TRUE)

We extract the results table from the proteins summarised experiment in the qf object.

inferencesIonUsage <- 
  msqrobCollect(qf[["ion_usage"]], L)
volcanoplot <- plotVolcano(inferencesIonUsage) + 
  facet_wrap(~contrast) + 
  labs(title = "Ion Usage")
volcanoplot

Developed by Lieven Clement, Nina Demeulemeester, Luca De Corso, Lucas Beerland.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
